design_philosophy.core_concept: Professional Network Operations Center (NOC) tool. High density, high contrast, precision data visualization.

buttons.default: bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90 text-white font-medium px-4 py-2 rounded-sm shadow-sm transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.outline: border border-input bg-background hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground rounded-sm

cards.classes: bg-card text-card-foreground rounded-sm border border-border shadow-sm p-5

inputs.classes: flex h-9 w-full rounded-sm border border-input bg-transparent px-3 py-1 text-sm shadow-sm transition-colors file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-1 focus-visible:ring-ring disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

tables.header_classes: h-10 px-2 text-left align-middle font-medium text-muted-foreground [&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0

visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-md bg-background/80 border-b border-border/50 sticky top-0 z-50
visual_enhancers.gradients: None. Use solid colors for professional clarity.

login_background.alt: Server rack lights in dark data center

login.left_panel: Brand logo, welcome text, feature highlights (Professional Network Monitoring)
login.right_panel: Login form (Username, Password), minimalist card centered

instructions_to_main_agent: Follow the 'Tactical Dark' theme strictly. No light mode toggles unless requested.
Use 'Rajdhani' font for all headings (h1-h4) and 'JetBrains Mono' for any data points (IP addresses, MAC addresses, Byte counts).
Use 'Inter' for standard UI text (labels, descriptions).
All tables must use 'dense' padding (p-2) to show maximum data.
Charts should use the defined 'status_colors' palette.
The Sidebar should be collapsible or thin icon-only on mobile.
Login page should feel like entering a secure facility.
Use Shadcn components but override rounded-lg with rounded-sm for a sharper, more technical look.
universal_guidelines: Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) for sticky headers.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable in layout containers, but tight spacing in data tables.
Subtle grain textures or noise overlays can be used on the background.
All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute.
